title: Spot Fleet Savings vs Interrupted Instance-Hours
spl:
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| search lineItem_UsageType="*SpotUsage*"
| stats sum(cost) as spot_spend by lineItem_UsageAccountId
| join type=left lineItem_UsageAccountId [
  search index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" earliest=-30d eventName="SpotInstanceInterruption"
  | eval acct=coalesce(recipientAccountId, accountId)
  | stats count as interruptions by acct
  | rename acct as lineItem_UsageAccountId
]
| fillnull value=0 interruptions
| eval savings_per_intr=if(interruptions>0, round(spend/interruptions,2), null())
| sort -spot_spend
description: Spot savings only matter if interruptions stay within SLOs. Correlating amortized spot spend with interruption counts from CloudTrail yields a simple dollars-saved-per-interruption metric so teams balance price and reliability across pools.
value: Spot savings only matter if interruptions stay within SLOs. Correlating amortized spot spend with interruption counts from CloudTrail yields a simple dollars-saved-per-interruption metric so teams balance price and reliability across pools.
implementation: (1) Normalize account IDs across billing and CloudTrail. (2) Schedule weekly review for accounts with high interruption counts and rising spot spend. (3) Pair with capacity-optimized vs price-optimized fleet settings from ASG/Launch Template metadata if ingested.
visualization: Scatter plot (spot spend vs interruptions), Table (accounts with worst ratio), Single value (fleet spot savings MTD).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk Add-on for AWS` (CUR + CloudTrail).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cloud_billing` `sourcetype="aws:billing:cur"`, `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize account IDs across billing and CloudTrail. (2) Schedule weekly review for accounts with high interruption counts and rising spot spend. (3) Pair with capacity-optimized vs price-optimized fleet settings from ASG/Launch Template metadata if ingested.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| search lineItem_UsageType="*SpotUsage*"
| stats sum(cost) as spot_spend by lineItem_UsageAccountId
| join type=left lineItem_UsageAccountId [
  search index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" earliest=-30d eventName="SpotInstanceInterruption"
  | eval acct=coalesce(recipientAccountId, accountId)
  | stats count as interruptions by acct
  | rename acct as lineItem_UsageAccountId
]
| fillnull value=0 interruptions
| eval savings_per_intr=if(interruptions>0, round(spend/interruptions,2), null())
| sort -spot_spend
```

Understanding this SPL

**Spot Fleet Savings vs Interrupted Instance-Hours** — Spot savings only matter if interruptions stay within SLOs. Correlating amortized spot spend with interruption counts from CloudTrail yields a simple dollars-saved-per-interruption metric so teams balance price and reliability across pools.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cloud_billing` `sourcetype="aws:billing:cur"`, `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk Add-on for AWS` (CUR + CloudTrail). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_billing; **sourcetype**: aws:billing:cur. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_billing, sourcetype="aws:billing:cur", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by lineItem_UsageAccountId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Fills null values with `fillnull`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **savings_per_intr** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Scatter plot (spot spend vs interruptions), Table (accounts with worst ratio), Single value (fleet spot savings MTD).
grandmaExplanation: Cost and capacity tools watch how much we spend on cloud and the data center, and where we have spare or strained capacity.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes from autoscaling events, new workload deployments, batch jobs, or DR drill resources.
